headline: Multi-@gpu_kernel-per-file source-to-silicon pipeline GENERALISES — one source emits one PTX with two .visible .entry, both kernels bit-exact under shared inputs

cross_check.note: LCG seed identical to N34 + N50 → element 0 of vec_mul C buffer is byte-identical to the standalone N50 fixture's c[0]. Cross-fire reproducibility evidence.

context.ptx_size_vs_2x_single:
4043 = ~14-byte header (`.version 7.0
.target sm_80
.address_size 64
`) + 2 entries; vec_add body 2026 bytes (N34=2428 included extra register decls trail) + vec_mul body 2017 bytes; deduplicates header
context.driver_binary: /tmp/hexa_n50_driver (1611936 B Mach-O arm64, build artifact from N50 cycle — same hexa_v2 self-host gen, reused since no compiler-source edits in this cycle)

ptxas info    : Used 16 registers, used 0 barriers

multi_kernel_generalisation_evidence.claim: MFunc partition + _nvptx_codegen iterator + emit_ptx joiner correctly handle N>1 @gpu_kernel functions in one source — no codegen edits required
verified_paths: Parse: 2 @gpu_kernel decls accepted by lex+parse+lower+lower_hir (single hexa parse-gate PASS)
Filter (nvptx_target.hexa:2391-2412): `while i < len(module.funcs)` keeps both gpu_kind=KERNEL MFuncs
Lower-per-MFunc: _nvptx_lower_func called twice with distinct mfn (different name + body op)
Emit (`_emit_ptx_versioned` / `emit_ptx`): header emitted ONCE, both LFunc bodies concatenated via `parts.push(_emit_ptx_func(module.funcs[i]))` loop
Module-level identifiers correctly scoped per kernel (param_0..3 prefix uses kernel name)
cuModuleLoadDataEx accepts multi-entry PTX (ptxas info shows TWO 'Compiling entry function' lines)
cuModuleGetFunction resolves both 'vec_add' and 'vec_mul' from one module handle
Independent launches do not cross-contaminate device state (each kernel allocates+frees its own da/db/dc)
FP64 add+mul bit-exact vs CPU ref (IEEE 754 correctly-rounded single ops, no FMA contraction since each kernel has only one arith op)
multi_kernel_generalisation_evidence.verdict: GENERALISED — source-to-silicon pipeline transparently handles multi-kernel modules. Bottleneck IS NOT a per-module single-kernel constraint.

honest_scope_g3.no_compiler_edits: Pure fixture + artifact cycle. No edits to compiler/, self/, stdlib/, or any source under codegen/. Verified: `git status -- compiler/ self/ stdlib/` clean delta vs HEAD on entry.
honest_scope_g3.driver_reuse: Reused /tmp/hexa_n50_driver (Mach-O arm64 from N50 cycle). PTX emission succeeds because Path B (_build_nvptx_emit_driver) was already wired in N35+N45 — N55 exercises THE SAME pipeline with an N=2-kernel input, no rebuild needed.
honest_scope_g3.ptx_size_observation: Header is 14 bytes single-instance. Per-kernel body ~2020 bytes. Multi-kernel PTX size scales near-linearly with kernel count (no per-kernel hidden global emission).
honest_scope_g3.ptxas_emit_order: ptxas-info shows vec_mul compiled BEFORE vec_add despite source order vec_add THEN vec_mul. This is ptxas internal scheduling — order in the .ptx text IS source order (vec_add at L6, vec_mul at L61). No semantic effect.
honest_scope_g3.cross_module_register_aliasing: Both kernels reuse the same SSA-style register names (%rd0..%fd18). Safe because each `.entry` body is a self-contained scope per PTX ISA — registers are per-function, not module-global. Confirmed by ptxas accepting + JIT compiling both.
honest_scope_g3.ipc_isolation: Per-kernel cuMemAlloc/Free in fire_kernel() means vec_mul does NOT inherit vec_add's device buffers. Each kernel gets fresh memset(0xee) sentinel; both produce PASS independently.
honest_scope_g3.no_gap_surfaced: F-RFC071-E2E-MULTI-KERNEL-NUMERIC-EQ PASS first try. No partial emit (e.g., 1-entry PTX), no missing cuModuleGetFunction handle, no numeric drift. Generalisation gap from N34/N50 single-kernel fixtures CLOSED.
